---
title: "about"
image: headshot-small.png
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: solana
  image-width: 10em
  image-alt: Headshot of Alex
  image-shape: rounded
---

:::{#hero-heading}
I am a Postdoctoral Scholar at Stanford University in the Economics Department, where I work with [Arun Chandrasekhar](https://stanford.edu/~arungc/). I study spectral methods for network analysis, causal inference, and causal inference on networks. In September 2026, I will join Oregon State University as an Assistant Professor in Statistics. 

I completed my PhD in Statistics at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, where I was co-advised by [Keith Levin](https://pages.stat.wisc.edu/~kdlevin/) and [Karl Rohe](http://pages.stat.wisc.edu/~karlrohe/). During my PhD I also spent some time working on [`broom`](https://broom.tidymodels.org/), a popular open-source `R` package in the `tidyverse`. 

My curriculum vitae is available [here](academic-cv.pdf). I also keep [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=6g1T3WIAAAAJ) up to date, and you can find me on Bluesky at [@alexpghayes.com](https://bsky.app/profile/alexpghayes.com).
:::

## recent updates

- 2026-04-16: [`vsp 0.1.4`](https://rohelab.github.io/vsp/) is on CRAN. This update adds several heuristic localization metrics useful for investigating the quality of estimated embeddings.

- 2026-04-15: [`spectralard 0.0.0.9000`](https://github.com/alexpghayes/spectralard) is on Github. This in-development package implements estimators for random dot product graphs from Aggregated Relational Data.

- 2026-04-11: I presented on [Spectral Estimation and Trait Selection for Aggregated Relational Data](https://github.com/alexpghayes/2026-04-11-nsec-2026) at Network Science in Economics 2026.

- 2026-04-09: I have a new working paper [Estimating peer effects in noisy, low-rank networks via network smoothing](./papers/latent-contagion-wp.pdf) with Keith Levin.

- 2026-04-06: I presented [Spectral Estimation and Trait Selection for Aggregated Relational Data](https://github.com/alexpghayes/2026-04-06-metrics-lunch) at the Stanford Metrics Lunch.

- 2025-12-15: I presented [Minimax rates for the linear-in-means model reveal an identifiability-estimability gap](https://github.com/alexpghayes/2025-12-15-cmstat) virtually at CMStatistics 2025.

- 2025-11-19: I presented my poster [Estimating how much people influence their friends when friendship\ measurements are unreliable](https://github.com/alexpghayes/2025-11-19-sc2-poster) at the Stanford Causal Science Center student conference.

- 2025-11-14: I presented early work [Mapping Social Networks on a Budget: Using Aggregated Relational Data in Development Economics](https://github.com/alexpghayes/2025-11-14-madison-networks-talk) at the UW-Madison Networks Seminar.

- 2025-11-04: I posted an updated draft [Minimax rates for the linear-in-means model reveal an identifiability-estimability gap](http://arxiv.org/abs/2410.10772) to ArXiV. This new draft generalizes our previous result loading bounding estimation error in ordinary least squares estimators for the linear-in-means model to a generic minimax lower bound that holds for all possible estimators.

- 2025-09-22: I presented [Minimax rates for the linear-in-means model reveal an identifiability-estimability gap](https://github.com/alexpghayes/2025-09-22-stanford-metrics-lunch) at the Stanford Metrics Lunch.

- 2025-09-01: I joined Stanford as a Postdoctoral Scholar working with Arun Chandrasekhar.

- 2025-08-07 I presented [Peer effects in the linear-in-means model may be inestimable even when identified](https://github.com/alexpghayes/2025-08-07-jsm-slides) at the Joint Statistical Meetings 2025.

- 2025-05-09: I graduated from the University of Wisconsin-Madison with a PhD in Statistics.

Material from older talks, presentations, posters, etc, can be found [on my Github](https://github.com/alexpghayes/).

## working papers & pre-prints

1. [Estimating peer effects in noisy, low-rank networks via network smoothing](./papers/latent-contagion-wp.pdf) (pdf). **Alex Hayes** and Keith Levin. April 10, 2026

1. [Minimax rates for the linear-in-means model reveal an identifiability-estimability gap](http://arxiv.org/abs/2410.10772). **Alex Hayes** and Keith Levin. *arXiv*. November 4, 2025. [replication package](https://github.com/alexpghayes/asymptotic-collinearity-replication)

## publications

1. [Estimating network-mediated causal effects via principal components network regression](http://jmlr.org/papers/v26/23-1317.html). **Alex Hayes**, Mark M. Fredrickson, and Keith Levin. *Journal of Machine Learning Research*. 2025.  [replication package](https://github.com/alexpghayes/network-mediation-replication), [code](https://github.com/alexpghayes/latentnetmediate)

1. [Co-factor analysis of citation networks](http://www.tandfonline.com/10.1080/10618600.2024.2394464). **Alex Hayes** and Karl Rohe. *Journal of Computational and Graphical Statistics*. 2024. [post-print](./papers/cofactor-analysis-of-citation-networks-aom.pdf), [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.14604), [replication package](https://github.com/alexpghayes/citation-cofactoring-replication), [code](https://rohelab.github.io/fastadi/)

1. [Welcome to the tidyverse](https://joss.theoj.org/papers/10.21105/joss.01686). Hadley Wickham, Mara Averick, Jennifer Bryan, Winston Chang, Lucy D'Agostino McGowan, Romain François, Garrett Grolemund, **Alex Hayes**, Lionel Henry, Jim Hester, Max Kuhn, Thomas Lin Pedersen, Evan Miller, Kirill Müller, David Robinson, Dana Paige Seidel, Vitalie Spinu, Kohske Takahashi, Davis Vaughan, Claus Wilke, Kara Woo, Hiroaki Yutani. *Journal of Open Source Software*. 2019. [website](https://www.tidyverse.org/)

## statistical consulting

At Stanford, I do some informal statistical consulting in the economics department, especially for grad students. [Learn more](./consulting/).

## blog

In a hobbyist capacity, I also blog about statistics, programming, and data. Some posts I enjoyed writing:

- [overfitting: a guided tour](./post/2020-01-06_overfitting-a-guide-tour/)
- [consistency and the linear probability model](./post/2019-08-31_consistency-and-the-linear-probability-model/)
- [fairness in repeated lotteries](./post/2024-06-09_repeated-lotteries/)

<!-- 

## students
- classes i'm teaching
- working with me / lab manual
- understanding what's happening in academia
- for prospective students -- research topics of interest

-->
